title: erigeron latus
intro: Erigeron latus, also known as Mexican daisy or Fleabane, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to Mexico and the southwestern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which can help to treat various health issues such as wounds, fever, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Erigeron latus include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various ailments. Erigeron latus can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall health and well-being.
benefits: Erigeron latus has many benefits, such as its high content of antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals, which have been shown to improve overall health by reducing inflammation, fighting cancer cells, and protecting against heart disease. Additionally, this plant is rich in flavonoids, which have been known to lower blood pressure, improve cognitive function, and reduce the risk of age-related diseases. The benefits of Erigeron latus also extend to its antibacterial and antifungal properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and infections, ultimately improving the quality of life for those who use it. By incorporating Erigeron latus into one's daily routine, individuals can experience improved physical and mental well-being, leading to a more vibrant and fulfilling life.
intro_study: According to "Zhong yao cai = Zhongyaocai = Journal of Chinese medicinal materials", erigeron latus (presumably referring to Erigeron breviscapus) may help with anticoagulation by delaying coagulation time and prothrombin time, inhibiting platelet factor III, and enhancing fibrinolysis.
